content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of NNN integers. Find the minimum number of integers you need to delete from the array such that the absolute difference between each pair of integers in the remaining array will become equal.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of integers to be deleted to satisfy the given condition.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one pair of integers and the absolute difference between them is ‚ą£A1‚ąíA2‚ą£=‚ą£1‚ąí2‚ą£=1|A_1 - A_2| = |1 - 2| = 1‚ą£A1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč‚ą£=‚ą£1‚ąí2‚ą£=1. So there is no need to delete any integer from the given array.
Test case 222: If the integers 111 and 555 are deleted, the array A becomes [2,2,2][2, 2, 2][2,2,2] and the absolute difference between each pair of integers is 000. There is no possible way to delete less than two integers to satisfy the given condition.
